title: Box Office: 'Civil War' Is Pulling Ahead of 'Abigail' With $11M+
text: Civil War is pulling ahead of Abigail at the box office with $11 million in its second weekend, while Abigail opened to $10.2 million domestically. Universal's vampire horror film Abigail had been projected to earn between $12-15 million in its opening weekend based on pre-release tracking. The film's actual opening came in slightly below expectations but still represents a respectable debut for the horror genre. Alex Garland's Civil War maintained strong momentum in its second weekend with a 57% drop, demonstrating strong audience retention and word-of-mouth. The weekend saw intense competition between adult-oriented films, with Abigail, Civil War, and The Ministry of Ungentlemanly Warfare all vying for audience attention. Abigail's $28 million budget suggested it had a reasonable chance of profitability over its full theatrical run, despite the below-forecast opening. The film received positive critical reviews with an 82% on Rotten Tomatoes, but the B CinemaScore indicated lukewarm audience reception. Industry observers noted that horror films continued to struggle at the 2024 box office, with several releases underperforming expectations.
